receivingCourses.title: Introduction to Programming and Computational Problem Solving - Accelerated Pace

courses.title: Introduction to Programming and Computational Problem Solving - Accelerated Pace

sbccArticulations.noArticulationReason: This course must be taken at the university after transfer

notes.content: DATED MATERIAL, SUBJECT TO CHANGE. PLEASE CONSULT CURRENT UCSD GENERAL CATALOG FOR ANY ADDITIONAL INFORMATION. Effective Fall 2018, major preparation will be required for this major. For details, visit: http://admissions.ucsd.edu/MajorPrep This program is designed for students with substantial interest in probability theory and statistics. It is useful preparation for many fields of employment as well as graduate school. Students who have completed C/C++ Programming may be able to use that course in place of one of the required Java Programming classes listed below. Students should speak with a UCSD Mathematics advisor. For more information please visit https://math.ucsd.edu/students/undergraduate/ma35-probability-statistics-b-s/ NOTE: If no 20E equivalency is listed below, students who have completed vector calculus material in their Calc III course must successfully petition this class for 20E equivalency. Petitions must include course syllabus, textbook information and details on the final exam (including modality). More information on the petition process can be found at the following site: https://www.math.ucsd.edu/students/undergraduate/petitions
notes.contentHtml:
DATED MATERIAL, SUBJECT TO CHANGE. PLEASE CONSULT CURRENT UCSD GENERAL CATALOG FOR ANY ADDITIONAL INFORMATION.


Effective Fall 2018, major preparation will be required for this major. For details, visit: http://admissions.ucsd.edu/MajorPrep


This program is designed for students with substantial interest in probability theory and statistics. It is useful preparation for many fields of employment as well as graduate school.


Students who have completed C/C++ Programming may be able to use that course in place of one of the required Java Programming classes listed below. Students should speak with a UCSD Mathematics advisor.


For more information please visit https://math.ucsd.edu/students/undergraduate/ma35-probability-statistics-b-s/


NOTE: If no 20E equivalency is listed below, students who have completed vector calculus material in their Calc III course must successfully petition this class for 20E equivalency. Petitions must include course syllabus, textbook information and details on the final exam (including modality). More information on the petition process can be found at the following site: https://www.math.ucsd.edu/students/undergraduate/petitions
